sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid When Using Build Calculators
sections.body:
Even with the best ad-free tool, it's easy to make errors. Here are three common pitfalls and how StatForge helps you avoid them:

  • Ignoring soft caps: Many games reduce the benefit of a stat after a certain point. For example, in World of Warcraft, haste has a soft cap at 30%—beyond that, each point gives only 0.5% of the original benefit. Our calculator displays a warning when you approach a soft cap.
  • Overlooking synergies: A build that looks great on paper may fail in practice if skills don't synergize. StatForge's synergy checker highlights when two abilities conflict or amplify each other. For example, a +20% fire damage bonus won't help if your main skill deals ice damage.
  • Not accounting for enemy defenses: Your DPS against a training dummy is different from real enemies. We include an 'enemy armor' slider that lets you simulate different mob types, from low-defense trash to high-defense bosses.

By avoiding these mistakes, you can create builds that are not only numerically optimal but also practical in actual gameplay. And since there are no ads, you can experiment freely without interruption.

  • Rogue: +1 Agility = +2.9 physical power + 1.2% action speed. +1 Strength = +2.2 physical power. Agility is king for burst damage.
